item.title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.33}{\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of Cu0.33TiSe2 across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in the normal state above the CD…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

item.title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.description:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta $\mathbf{k}$ and $−\mathbf{k}$. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multipl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta k and −k. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multiple metallic, and Mott-i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.dc:creator: Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: From hidden order to skyrmions: Quantum Hall states in an extended Hofstadter-Fermi-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): F. J. Pauw, U. Schollwöck, N. Goldman, S. Paeckel, and F. A. Palm

The interplay between topology and strong interactions gives rise to a variety of exotic quantum phases, including fractional quantum Hall states and their lattice analogs—fractional Chern insulators (FCIs). Such topologically ordered states host fractionalized excitations and, for spinful systems, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035142] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. J. Pauw, U. Schollwöck, N. Goldman, S. Paeckel, and F. A. Palm

The interplay between topology and strong interactions gives rise to a variety of exotic quantum phases, including fractional quantum Hall states and their lattice analogs—fractional Chern insulators (FCIs). Such topologically ordered states host fractionalized excitations and, for spinful systems, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035142] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: From hidden order to skyrmions: Quantum Hall states in an extended Hofstadter-Fermi-Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: F. J. Pauw, U. Schollwöck, N. Goldman, S. Paeckel, and F. A. Palm

item.title: Photoinduced excitonic magnetism in a multiorbital Hubbard system
item.description:
Author(s): Lei Geng, Sujay Ray, and Philipp Werner

Multiorbital Hubbard models with Hund coupling and crystal-field splitting exhibit an instability toward spin-triplet excitonic order in the parameter regime characterized by strong local spin fluctuations. Upon chemical doping, two distinct types of excitonic ferromagnetism have been reported. Usin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035143] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lei Geng, Sujay Ray, and Philipp Werner

Multiorbital Hubbard models with Hund coupling and crystal-field splitting exhibit an instability toward spin-triplet excitonic order in the parameter regime characterized by strong local spin fluctuations. Upon chemical doping, two distinct types of excitonic ferromagnetism have been reported. Usin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035143] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Photoinduced excitonic magnetism in a multiorbital Hubbard system

item.title: Emergent topology of flat bands in a twisted bilayer $α\text{−}{T}_{3}$ lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Gourab Paul, Srijata Lahiri, Kuntal Bhattacharyya, and Saurabh Basu

We provide theoretical evidence of the emergent topology in a twisted bilayer system, despite the corresponding monolayer hosting topologically trivial properties. To this end, we investigate an interesting interplay of destructive interference due to lattice geometry and band folding due to enlarge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035145] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gourab Paul, Srijata Lahiri, Kuntal Bhattacharyya, and Saurabh Basu

We provide theoretical evidence of the emergent topology in a twisted bilayer system, despite the corresponding monolayer hosting topologically trivial properties. To this end, we investigate an interesting interplay of destructive interference due to lattice geometry and band folding due to enlarge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035145] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent topology of flat bands in a twisted bilayer $α\text{−}{T}_{3}$ lattice

item.title: Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident beam on a magneto-optical metagrating
item.description:
Author(s): Ma Luo

The Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident optical beam to a metagrating consisting of magneto-optical material is studied. The metagrating consists of a compound grating of magneto-optical rods atop a dielectric slab, which features staggered lateral shifts of the rods. The compound grating indu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035433] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ma Luo

The Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident optical beam to a metagrating consisting of magneto-optical material is studied. The metagrating consists of a compound grating of magneto-optical rods atop a dielectric slab, which features staggered lateral shifts of the rods. The compound grating indu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035433] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident beam on a magneto-optical metagrating

item.title: Dynamical signatures and control of time-reversal symmetry breaking in twisted nodal superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Jefferson Tang and Pavel A. Volkov

Recent observations of time-reversal breaking superconductivity at twisted cuprate interfaces motivate the development of new approaches to better characterize this emergent phenomenon. Here we study the dynamical properties of the order parameters at the twisted unconventional superconductor interf…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014513] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jefferson Tang and Pavel A. Volkov

Recent observations of time-reversal breaking superconductivity at twisted cuprate interfaces motivate the development of new approaches to better characterize this emergent phenomenon. Here we study the dynamical properties of the order parameters at the twisted unconventional superconductor interf…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014513] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.title: Nonequilibrium statistics of a biased Kondo resonance
item.description:
Author(s): Jong E. Han

Numerical renormalization group (NRG) is formulated for nonequilibrium steady state by converting finite-lattice many-body eigenstates into scattering states. Extension of the full-density-matrix NRG for a biased Anderson impurity model, simplified by using the original orbital basis of the Hamilton…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045141] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jong E. Han

Numerical renormalization group (NRG) is formulated for nonequilibrium steady state by converting finite-lattice many-body eigenstates into scattering states. Extension of the full-density-matrix NRG for a biased Anderson impurity model, simplified by using the original orbital basis of the Hamilton…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045141] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.title: Multiple quantum spin Hall states and topological current divider in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Hao He, Zhao Gong, Shuai Li, Jian-Jun Liu, Hui-Ying Mu, and Xing-Tao An

Recent studies have demonstrated that topological quantum spin Hall (QSH) states exist in twisted bilayers of transition metal dichalcogenides. However, a comprehensive theoretical characterization of the topological edge states remains a topic of interest and an unresolved issue. Here, the topologi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045423] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao He, Zhao Gong, Shuai Li, Jian-Jun Liu, Hui-Ying Mu, and Xing-Tao An

Recent studies have demonstrated that topological quantum spin Hall (QSH) states exist in twisted bilayers of transition metal dichalcogenides. However, a comprehensive theoretical characterization of the topological edge states remains a topic of interest and an unresolved issue. Here, the topologi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045423] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Multiple quantum spin Hall states and topological current divider in twisted bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Single crystal growth, structural and physical properties, and absence of a charge density wave in ${\mathrm{Ti}}_{0.85}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{6}{\mathrm{Ge}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Dechao Cheng, Nour Maraytta, Xiuhua Chen, Xizhi Li, Xueliang Wu, Xiangxiang Jing, Yong Hu, Youpin Gong, Mingquan He, Yisheng Chai, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Pengfei Jiang, Yilin Wang, Michael Merz, and Aifeng Wang

The rattling chain model predicts CDWs in AM6X6 kagome compounds with small filler atoms A embedded in a large M6X6 host. Here, despite Ti being a small filler atom, the authors observe no CDW in Ti0.85Fe6Ge6. Through in-depth electronic structure and bonding analyses, they demonstrate that, in addition to ionic radius, the bonding characteristics of the filler atom are crucial for CDW formation. This finding significantly extends the rattling chain model, offering new insights into the factors driving CDW transitions in kagome materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035133] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dechao Cheng, Nour Maraytta, Xiuhua Chen, Xizhi Li, Xueliang Wu, Xiangxiang Jing, Yong Hu, Youpin Gong, Mingquan He, Yisheng Chai, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Pengfei Jiang, Yilin Wang, Michael Merz, and Aifeng Wang

The rattling chain model predicts CDWs in AM6X6 kagome compounds with small filler atoms A embedded in a large M6X6 host. Here, despite Ti being a small filler atom, the authors observe no CDW in Ti0.85Fe6Ge6. Through in-depth electronic structure and bonding analyses, they demonstrate that, in addition to ionic radius, the bonding characteristics of the filler atom are crucial for CDW formation. This finding significantly extends the rattling chain model, offering new insights into the factors driving CDW transitions in kagome materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035133] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Single crystal growth, structural and physical properties, and absence of a charge density wave in ${\mathrm{Ti}}_{0.85}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{6}{\mathrm{Ge}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: Dechao Cheng, Nour Maraytta, Xiuhua Chen, Xizhi Li, Xueliang Wu, Xiangxiang Jing, Yong Hu, Youpin Gong, Mingquan He, Yisheng Chai, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Pengfei Jiang, Yilin Wang, Michael Merz, and Aifeng Wang

item.title: Filling-dependent phase competition in the interacting kagome flat band system
item.description:
Author(s): Kevin J. U. Vidarte, R. Cardias, and A. Latgé

The kagome lattice, known for its geometric frustration and flat bands, provides a fertile ground for emerging correlated electronic phases. Using a self-consistent mean-field approach within the extended Hubbard model, we compute the phase diagrams of the kagome lattice as a function of on-site and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035134] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kevin J. U. Vidarte, R. Cardias, and A. Latgé

The kagome lattice, known for its geometric frustration and flat bands, provides a fertile ground for emerging correlated electronic phases. Using a self-consistent mean-field approach within the extended Hubbard model, we compute the phase diagrams of the kagome lattice as a function of on-site and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035134] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Filling-dependent phase competition in the interacting kagome flat band system
item.dc:creator: Kevin J. U. Vidarte, R. Cardias, and A. Latgé
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Engineering Hubbard models with gated two-dimensional moiré systems
item.description:
Author(s): Yiqi Yang (杨怡奇), Yubo Yang (杨煜波), Miguel A. Morales, and Shiwei Zhang

Lattice models are powerful tools for studying strongly correlated quantum many-body systems, but their general lack of exact solutions motivates efforts to simulate them in tunable platforms. Recently, a promising new candidate has emerged for such platforms from two-dimensional materials. A subset…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035135] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yiqi Yang (杨怡奇), Yubo Yang (杨煜波), Miguel A. Morales, and Shiwei Zhang

Lattice models are powerful tools for studying strongly correlated quantum many-body systems, but their general lack of exact solutions motivates efforts to simulate them in tunable platforms. Recently, a promising new candidate has emerged for such platforms from two-dimensional materials. A subset…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035135] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Engineering Hubbard models with gated two-dimensional moiré systems
item.dc:creator: Yiqi Yang (杨怡奇), Yubo Yang (杨煜波), Miguel A. Morales, and Shiwei Zhang

item.title: Phases and criticality of the triangular lattice $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Lu Zhang, Rongning Liu, and Xue-Yang Song

We report the study of phases and transitions of the $\text{SU}(N)$ Hofstadter-Hubbard model subject to a commensurate magnetic field on the triangular lattice. At filling one fermion per site, for the number of fermion flavors $2≤N≤8$, we identify three distinct phases and calculate critical intera…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045131] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lu Zhang, Rongning Liu, and Xue-Yang Song

We report the study of phases and transitions of the SU(N) Hofstadter-Hubbard model subject to a commensurate magnetic field on the triangular lattice. At filling one fermion per site, for the number of fermion flavors 2≤N≤8, we identify three distinct phases and calculate critical interaction stren…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045131] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Phases and criticality of the triangular lattice $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Lu Zhang, Rongning Liu, and Xue-Yang Song
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Emergent magnetism and spin liquids in an extended Hubbard model at fractional filling
item.description:
Author(s): Zhenhao Song, Urban F. P. Seifert, Leon Balents, and Hong-Chen Jiang

Motivated by twisted transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), we study an extended Hubbard model with both onsite and off-site repulsive interactions, in which Mott insulating states with concomitant charge order occur at fractional fillings. To resolve the charge ordering as well as the fate of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045132] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhenhao Song, Urban F. P. Seifert, Leon Balents, and Hong-Chen Jiang

Motivated by twisted transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), we study an extended Hubbard model with both onsite and off-site repulsive interactions, in which Mott insulating states with concomitant charge order occur at fractional fillings. To resolve the charge ordering as well as the fate of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045132] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent magnetism and spin liquids in an extended Hubbard model at fractional filling
item.dc:creator: Zhenhao Song, Urban F. P. Seifert, Leon Balents, and Hong-Chen Jiang
